Built multiple production AI systems including GenAI-Intel (cloud market intelligence platform), Forge (AI interview prep), and internal AWS agent tooling. Combines deep business strategy experience with hands-on AI product development.
summary: Senior GTM and operations leader who has spent the last 6.5 years building data and intelligence systems at AWS — and the last 2 building AI products on top of them, both inside Amazon and independently. I bring rare fluency across business strategy, AI infrastructure, and hands-on product building: I understand the customer problem deeply enough to architect the solution, and I ship it myself. Two live products. One heading to production inside AWS.
education: MBA, W.P. Carey / ASU; B.Tech EE, BPUT

projects.title: GenAI-Intel
projects.subtitle: Automated VC Intelligence System
projects.description: Built to automate what I was doing manually at AWS — identifying which VC-funded startups are running on which cloud and AI providers, and prioritizing which ones to engage. A live AI intelligence platform tracking 110+ VC-funded startups with cloud and AI provider attribution, vertical classification, and engagement prioritization. Core: a 4-tier attribution engine (deterministic signals → dual LLM fallback via Perplexity Sonar + Claude Haiku) with confidence scoring and entrenchment ratings. Phase 1 added vertical/sub-vertical classification across 84 sub-verticals with cloud propensity tags (High/Medium/Low). Phase 2 added engagement priority tiers (Engage Now / Watch / Track), trigger detection (hiring surges, leadership changes, product launches), and LLM-generated outreach intelligence in a dedicated “Ready to Engage” view. Built end-to-end using Claude Code without a traditional software engineering background.
projects.stack: FastAPI, Python, Next.js, Supabase, Claude API (Anthropic), Claude Code, Perplexity Sonar, Brave Search API, Railway, Vercel
projects.outcome: Live at cloud-intel.vercel.app. Daily automated pipeline; 110+ startups tracked with cloud/AI attribution, vertical classification, propensity tags, engagement tiers, trigger detection, and LLM-generated outreach intelligence. Full product across 5 dashboard views.

projects.title: Forge
projects.subtitle: Communication OS
projects.description: Daily practice platform for professional writing and speaking. Two loops: (1) writing scored by Claude Sonnet across 4 dimensions (clarity, structure, concision, persuasion), (2) speaking via OpenAI Whisper transcription + AI delivery analysis. Tracks skill growth via a custom SVG radar chart, streaks, and on-demand coaching notes. Full auth flow, RLS-protected Supabase database, 6-table schema, and a clean indigo design system.
projects.stack: Next.js 16, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, Supabase, Claude API (Anthropic), OpenAI Whisper, Vercel

projects.title: AI Executive Reporting Toolkit
projects.subtitle: Internal AWS (Startups Org)
projects.description: Architected a multi-agent AI system powering two CEO reporting cadences: bi-weekly (top newly-funded startups) and quarterly (top-funded across 18 months). Each report delivers a 360-degree view — internal metrics, CRM engagement, external funding data, customer snapshots, and primary cloud and AI provider inference.
projects.stack: Amazon Q, MCP (Salesforce), Python, AWS Bedrock, Claude API, Perplexity, internal APIs
agents: (1) Internal metrics — Amazon Q agent querying internal data tables via natural language. Outputs data for 1,000+ customers in a single run; saves 40+ analyst hours per quarter.
(2) CRM engagement — Pioneered an Amazon Q agent with MCP server connected to internal Salesforce. Per-account engagement summaries and next steps at near-instant speed; saves 80+ hours per cycle.
(3) External data — Web-scanning agent generating customer snapshots and funding summaries. Produces in 15 mins what took 40+ analyst hours. Scaling with product team to support 500+ customers.

(4) Primary Cloud Provider (PCP) — Adopted by AWS startup leadership and the CEO for executive reporting and ad-hoc searches. Built through 50+ iterations using both web signals and internal CRM data. Outputs three tiers: web inference, internal data inference, and consolidated PCP.
(5) Primary AI Provider — Extended the PCP agent to infer primary AI provider using the same dual-signal approach.

(6) Vertical classification agent — Built an agent to scan startup websites and tag each company’s vertical and sub-vertical against a pre-defined taxonomy, enabling cloud propensity to serve as a filter alongside funding signals.

work_history.title: Sr. Sales Operations Manager — Startups
bullets: Pioneered use of PCP framework for tracking AWS market share efficacy; elevated PCP to a key organizational success metric now tracked at the S-team level and influencing S-team goal revisions.

Led product definition and cross-functional development of Startup Pulse — AWS’s first AI-powered PCP and primary AI provider identification system, now in productionization with Data and Software Engineering. Achieved 10x scale improvement in analysis capacity through GenAI automation; saves 70% of report generation time.
Launched and owned the Monthly Top Startups Report for Matt Garman’s office (Q1 2024) — developed ranking logic across 7 cohorts covering 175 high-potential startups.
Delivered the Top 100 AI Startups report for Andy Jassy’s office — developed and refined the ranking logic, coordinated with 130+ account teams.
Led the OP1 workstream for the Startups org — developed the Market Summary tab for executive visibility, generated PCP analysis for all OP1-listed startups.
Applied the Startup Pulse prototype to generate the top startups engagement list for AWS re:Invent — used directly by AWS CEO Matt Garman.
Originated and socialized the strategic framework that vertical classification — not funding size alone — should be the primary filter for identifying high-potential startups.
Built GenAI applications to automate multi-step executive reporting workflows, integrating Claude, OpenAI, and Perplexity to replace manual analyst processes.
Led cross-functional data integration initiatives to unlock new internal use cases, evangelizing GenAI adoption beyond dashboards to the full leadership org.
Partnered with the AWS Credits team to develop a data-driven scoring mechanism for startup credit allocation.

work_history.title: Go-to-Market Strategy Manager — Startups
bullets: Built the data infrastructure and GTM tooling that became the foundation for the intelligence systems developed in the Sr. Sales Operations Manager role.
Designed and built the Startup 360 mechanism from scratch — driving a 70% increase in usage over 6 months (396 to 667 users) and earning a top-3 ranking in seller productivity surveys.
Onboarded 6 net-new third-party data vendors, adding 100k+ data points across 20k+ accounts with targeted coverage for EEM, ASEAN, and LATAM regions.
Built campaign performance and MBR self-service dashboards, saving 16+ cumulative hours per month across geo teams.
Built a self-service Triggers dashboard tracking 50k+ monthly engagement signals in near real-time.
Defined the MVP business use cases for the Market Intelligence Product and led development of the historical funding business logic.

work_history.title: Business Intelligence Engineer — Partner Org
bullets: Designed and built the Partner Productivity dashboard to track role-based productivity across KPIs and granularities.
Led development and implementation of logic for Partner Revenue 2.0 with leadership, partner sales, and systems teams.
Built and maintained ETL pipelines using Cradle, Redshift, and S3; reduced manual reporting time by 8 hrs/week.
Rebuilt the Total Addressable Spend (TAS) model, improving prediction accuracy for high-value customers from 20% to 80% — a 4x improvement.
Identified and fixed a systemic 3P data quality issue through deduplication logic across 20k+ accounts — correcting a $391M (15%) overstatement in monthly TAS model value.

work_history.title: Institutional Sales Manager
bullets: Managed $28M+ B2B sales portfolio serving MARS, Siemens, GE, and AkzoNobel; grew revenue 182% over 14 months.

bullets: QA and systems testing for enterprise Java applications; Selenium-based automated testing, PostgreSQL, and UNIX environments.
